mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rate near zero anchors short-term discount rates and suppresses volatility, creating a consistent tailwind for equity valuations over short horizons.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as consumer demand weakness translates into earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 55% of months where RSAFS month-over-month decline exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in consumer prices alongside a near-zero federal funds rate generates negative real interest rates that drive capital reallocation into equities, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the joint z-score of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or average 60-day returns are not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes, month-over-month growth in industrial production signals supply-side recovery without immediate overheating risk, leading to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 8%, positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not produce positive average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate reflect improving labor market conditions that reduce household income risk and support consumption-driven corporate earnings, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Months with declining UNRATE do not produce positive average 60-day returns over any 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate trades below 0.25%, liquidity trap conditions suppress real yields and trigger flight-to-equity flows, producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad demand acceleration that exceeds current productive capacity, driving positive earnings revisions and 20-day equity gains.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of concurrent instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing momentum that drives operating leverage and margin expansion, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not correlate with positive 60-day returns, evidenced by Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
